title: Crispy Umami American Chicken Fried Rice
description: A nostalgic American dish featuring crispy, umami-rich chicken and rice, perfect for a diabetic-friendly meal. This recipe uses brown rice and controlled sugar content to make it a healthier option.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned and crispy, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, then add the mushrooms and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the scallions to the skillet and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Push the mushroom and scallion mixture to one side of the skillet.
Pour the beaten eggs into the other side of the skillet and scramble them until cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
Mix the eggs with the mushroom and scallion mixture.
Add the cooked brown rice to the skillet, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper, and cook for about 2 minutes, until the rice is well combined with the other ingredients.
Add the cooked chicken back into the skillet and stir to combine with the rice mixture.
Deglaze the skillet with a small amount of water if necessary, to get all the crispy bits from the bottom.
